title: Switch to using libnetwork's resolvconf package
body:
Libnetwork provides a well-tested package for generating resolv.conf from the host's that has some features our current implementation does not. Swap to using their code and remove our built-in implementation.

Fixes #1533

comments.body:
Alright, they're still on the old Sirupsen package in the tag I used, but not master.

Let me bump to master...
Alright, the vendoring situation is getting absurd. Going to move the code into a pkg/ dir in our repo.
Failing cloud podman is due to Podman 0.9.3 making its way into the crio-tested repo, so we're pulling the version with the bug we fixed in 0.9.3.1

Looks like some process has been killed in Jenkins (-> restarting just the failing build).
@mheon, looks like some integration tests are unhappy.

Changes LGTM, I suspect some of the userns tests may need some tweaking though:

```
/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/run_userns_test.go:11
  podman uidmapping and gidmapping --net=host [It]
  /go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/run_userns_test.go:68

  Expected
      <int>: 127
  to equal
      <int>: 0
```
Pushed again, integration tests should hopefully be fixed.
